nodes.name: New Caledonian island thrush
nodes.summary: New Caledonian island thrush（New Caledonian island thrush，学名 Turdus xanthopus），在图谱中标记为濒危（EN）物种，主要分布于熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林、格朗德特爾島 (新喀里多尼亞)，主要威胁包括小种群风险。
locations: 熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林
格朗德特爾島 (新喀里多尼亞)

nodes.name: 小种群风险
nodes.summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。

nodes.id: loc-熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林-dbaa79b4
nodes.name: 熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林
nodes.summary: 熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联26种鸟类，包括銹紅林鷓鴣、Key West quail-dove、粉顶果鸠等26种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: EN
nodes.summary: EN表示 IUCN 濒危等级，当前关联384种鸟类，如朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭、黑脸琵鹭等384种鸟类。

nodes.id: loc-格朗德特爾島-新喀里多尼亞-ffab91bc
nodes.name: 格朗德特爾島 (新喀里多尼亞)
nodes.summary: 格朗德特爾島 (新喀里多尼亞)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联3种鸟类，包括新喀里多尼亞鸚鵡、新喀鸦、New Caledonian island thrush，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.target: loc-熱帶和亞熱帶旱生闊葉林-dbaa79b4
links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The New Caledonian island thrush used to have a large distribution in New Caledonia on Grande Terre, where it was mainly found in dry forests, particularly on the west coast of the island. The last confirmed sighting of the bird on Grande Terre was in 1928 and it was considered extinct until being rediscovered on the islands of Yandé and Néba in 1978

links.key: bird-new-caledonian-island-thrush-a55684a0__distributed_in__loc-格朗德特爾島-新喀里多尼亞-ffab91bc
links.target: loc-格朗德特爾島-新喀里多尼亞-ffab91bc
links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The New Caledonian island thrush used to have a large distribution in New Caledonia on Grande Terre, where it was mainly found in dry forests, particularly on the west coast of the island. The last confirmed sighting of the bird on Grande Terre was in 1928 and it was considered extinct until being rediscovered on the islands of Yandé and Néba in 1978

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: But owing to its small current distribution and significant loss of historical range – formerly being found on Grande Terre before being extirpated – along with its small population size and vulnerability, it is very likely to be endangered. Following its rediscovery on Yandé, the population was estimated to be around 100 individuals, however in 2009 only 60 individuals were recorded in a census. A census on the island of Néba from 2015 to 2016 had an estimate of less than 75 individuals. Current threats also include the degradation of its preferred habitat, as well as the risk of Black rat or New Caledonian crow being introduced to the islands, additionally both Pacific rat and feral cats are found on both islands. In 2019 the estimated suitable habitat on the islands was on Yandé, and on Néba
